concern.description: Required (non-null) when status is 'blocked': what the contract asks for, what makes it impossible as specified, and what would need to change in the plan. Always null when status is 'implemented'.

assumptions.description: Optional. Smallest-reasonable-assumption notes made to resolve a contract ambiguity (not a codebase-understanding question).

blocked_on.description: Optional, meaningful only when status is 'blocked'. 'contract' (the default if omitted): the contract itself is unsatisfiable as specified. 'tests' (implementor only, TDD): the implementor is confident the authored tests — not the contract — contradict the contract; the driver routes this back to the test author instead of retrying the implementor. Not schema-enforced against status, matching this schema's existing concern/status convention.

red_expected.description: Optional, test_implementor only, meaningful only when status is 'implemented' AND the driver's one-time RED gate (state.red_phase) is still pending — i.e. on the first authoring pass or any red_not_confirmed re-entry, never after RED has been confirmed or skipped once in this run. Defaults to true (the normal case — these tests should fail without an implementation). Set to false ONLY when EVERY test authored in this pass targets behavior that already exists in the codebase (e.g. added regression/coverage tests, no new implementation-requiring AC in this same pass) — the driver then skips the RED-confirmation tester run entirely and proceeds straight to the GREEN test run. Not schema-enforced against status or against red_phase, matching this schema's existing concern/blocked_on convention.
